front: What are the best practices for running Iceberg on S3?
back: S3 is strongly consistent since December 2020 (read-after-write and list-after-write consistency for all regions), which is a prerequisite for safe Iceberg operations. Key best practices: (1) Use path-style access (`s3://bucket/warehouse/db/table/`) — avoid virtual-hosted-style for compatibility. (2) Enable S3 versioning on the warehouse bucket for additional protection against accidental metadata corruption. (3) Use S3 object tagging or lifecycle rules to manage storage classes — compacted data to S3 Standard, old snapshots to S3 Infrequent Access. (4) Set appropriate S3 request retry settings in the Hadoop S3A client to handle transient errors. (5) Use S3 multi-part upload for large files (automatically used by the Hadoop S3A connector for files >100MB). (6) IAM permissions: writers need s3:PutObject, s3:DeleteObject, s3:GetObject; readers need s3:GetObject, s3:ListBucket. (7) Enable S3 Server-Side Encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS) for data at rest. (8) For write-heavy workloads, consider S3 Express One Zone for lower-latency metadata operations at higher cost.
